title: First 'jjf pull' from a fresh clone exits 1 (meta/format-version divergence)
body:
# Symptom

After cloning a jjforge project and running `jjf init`, the
first `jjf pull origin` exits 1 with:

```
jjf: invalid input: diverged ref refs/jjf/meta/format-version
not in a known v3 namespace (stem=meta/format-version);
refusing to merge
```

The actual data (issues, memories) DOES land, but the exit
code breaks any automation that gates on success.

# Repro

```
$ jj git clone --colocate central.git bob
$ cd bob && jjf init
$ jjf pull origin
jjf: invalid input: diverged ref refs/jjf/meta/format-version
not in a known v3 namespace (stem=meta/format-version);
refusing to merge
$ echo $?
1
$ jjf ls    # data is there
6cd59a5  open  ...  Alice's bug
```

# Root cause

`jjf init` writes a fresh `refs/jjf/meta/format-version` commit.
When the pull fetches the remote's `format-version` (different
SHA but logically same value), the per-ref merge driver refuses
to merge because `meta/` is outside the known v3 namespaces
(`issues/`, `memories/`).

# Fix candidates

1. Exclude `refs/jjf/meta/*` from the per-ref merge consideration
   — the meta refs are content-addressed metadata; either side's
   value is acceptable.
2. First-writer-wins on `meta/*` (prefer remote on conflict, or
   skip them entirely).
3. Delete `format-version` from the protocol if it isn't being
   read (see F-010).

# Cross-links

Same root cause as F-001 / F-003 / F-010. See epic for the
overall plan.

# Confidence

Reproducible 100% on every fresh clone. Tested in
`scratchpad/qa/r04-sync/bob`.
